description: Controlled vocabulary of behaviors the system can recognize and reason about. Maps onto network nodes for the network head. v1 ships eating_disorder pack; future condition packs extend without schema change. See idea.md §4.2, §7.3.

behaviors.label: Restriction episode
behaviors.description: Eating less than physically needed, skipping meals, restricting food groups or volume beyond medical guidance.

behaviors.label: Binge episode
behaviors.description: Eating in a discrete period an amount of food larger than most would eat, with a sense of loss of control.

behaviors.label: Compulsive exercise
behaviors.description: Exercise that feels driven, that the user feels unable to skip, or that is performed primarily to compensate for eating.

behaviors.label: Body checking
behaviors.description: Repeatedly examining specific body parts in mirrors, pinching, measuring, photographing, etc.

behaviors.label: Weight checking
behaviors.description: Self-weighing, particularly if frequent or context-bound (before/after meals, daily, etc.).

behaviors.label: Food rule followed
behaviors.description: A self-imposed rule about what/when/how to eat that was followed in this episode.

behaviors.label: Food rule broken
behaviors.description: A self-imposed rule about what/when/how to eat that was broken in this episode. Stance often emotionally loaded.

behaviors.label: Social eating moment
behaviors.description: Eating with others, eating in public, or notably avoiding either.

behaviors.label: Avoidance of food situation
behaviors.description: Declining or leaving a meal, gathering, or context involving food.

behaviors.label: Extended fasting
behaviors.description: Prolonged periods without intake. Distinct from a missed meal — refers to deliberate extended deprivation.

general_egosyntonic.label: General egosyntonic (cross-condition)
general_egosyntonic.description: Behaviors that may be valued by the person but cause harm or constrain life. For users whose condition pack is not eating-disorder-specific but for whom MI exploration of behavior function is useful.

behaviors.label: Compulsive checking
behaviors.description: Repeated checking behaviors (locks, appliances, body, social media) that feel necessary but cause distress or take significant time.

behaviors.label: Compulsive reassurance seeking
behaviors.description: Repeatedly asking others for confirmation about a worry, with relief that does not last.

behaviors.label: Overworking
behaviors.description: Working beyond what is sustainable, framed by the user as productivity or dedication, with cost to rest/health/relationships.

behaviors.label: Social withdrawal
behaviors.description: Reducing contact with others, framed as needing space or independence, beyond what the user finds restorative.

behaviors.label: Perfectionistic redoing
behaviors.description: Repeating or refusing to complete tasks until they meet an internal standard that may be unreachable.
